Wormwood, a perennial herb of the daisy family, is a plant that grows in temperate regions of Europe, Asia, and North Africa.

The health benefits of Wormwood include its antimicrobial and antiparasitic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, fever, and certain parasitic infections. Its extracts have also been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.

The bioactive constituents of Wormwood include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and volatile oils, particularly thujone, which is responsible for its medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Wormwood include teas, tinctures, and extracts, often used to treat digestive issues and as a natural remedy for certain parasitic infections.
